creator: pyunyh@gmail.com
text:
On Tue, Sep 28, 2010 at 12:20:37PM -0700, Pyun YongHyeon wrote:
> On Tue, Sep 28, 2010 at 06:40:06AM +0000, Leon Swanepoel - MWEB wrote:
> > The following reply was made to PR kern/120791; it has been noted by GNATS.
> > 
> > From: Leon Swanepoel - MWEB <LSwanepoel@mweb.com>
> > To: "'bug-followup@FreeBSD.org'" <bug-followup@FreeBSD.org>, Leon Swanepoel -
> >  MWEB <LSwanepoel@mweb.com>
> > Cc:  
> > Subject: Re: kern/120791: [bge] The Broadcom BCM5703 A2 NIC is running a lot
> >  of Ierrs.
> > Date: Tue, 28 Sep 2010 08:20:16 +0200
> > 
> 
> [...]
> 
> >  Yes I believe where are still experiencing the problems on Freebsd 8.0. A=
> >  pparently the developer of the driver stopped doing anymore dev on it and=
> >  =20noone else has bothered. So yay!
> >  
> 
> Ok, BCM5703 supports hardware MAC counters so I'd like to see it.
> Show me the output of "sysctl dev.bge.0.stats" on recent FreeBSD
> release as well as verbose dmesg output. If you have way to trigger
> the issue, please let me know how you triggered it.
> Note, you should use fairly recent FreeBSD release as there had
> been many improvements over the years on bge(4) and PHY handling.
> Forgot one thing, please also show me the output "ifconfig bge0".

Any progress?
